summary_text: { "score": 1, "vulnerabilities": [ { "severity": "high", "type": "authentication/authorization", "description": "Trusted GitHub authors are allowed to reach autonomous execution whenever the task stays below Tier 3, but intent and tier are chosen by first-match substring keywords in /Users/masayuki/.claude/auto-task/lib/task-parser.js:50-68 and /Users/masayuki/.claude/auto-task/decision-table.json. A destructive request that avoids obvious keywords like deploy/production can still fall into Tier 2 or default execution, and /Users/masayuki/.claude/auto-task/lib/task-executor.js:111-116 forwards the full issue title/body to delegate scripts without a stricter policy gate.", "remediation": "Replace keyword matching with deny-by-default intent schemas and a second policy layer that scores destructive verbs, repo/file scope, shell intent, and external side effects. Require explicit human approval for execution-capable tasks, even from trusted authors, unless they match a narrowly allowlisted safe class." }, { "severity": "high", "type": "authentication/authorization", "description": "The verification control weakens its own safety signal by rewriting high-risk terms such as production, main, credentials, delete all, and rm -rf before voting in /Users/masayuki/.claude/auto-task/lib/problem-solver.js:278-344. Because verification then runs with riskLevel 2 and claudeVote fixed to APPROVE, risky execution results can be under-classified and incorrectly accepted or auto-closed.", "remediation": "Do not sanitize safety-relevant tokens before control-plane review. Preserve raw evidence for the verifier, handle false positives with better rule design instead of text mutation, and raise verification risk dynamically when output includes destructive, credential, or environment-targeting indicators." }, { "severity": "medium", "type": "sensitive data exposure", "description": "Execution output is posted back to GitHub after regex masking in /Users/masayuki/.claude/auto-task/lib/task-reporter.js:16-40 and /Users/masayuki/.claude/auto-task/lib/problem-solver.js:114-124,249-260. This redaction is heuristic and can miss bearer tokens, signed URLs, custom key formats, multiline credentials, or tool-specific secrets, so sensitive data can still leak into issue comments.", "remediation": "Do not publish raw stdout/stderr by default. Keep full output local, post only structured summaries, and run a stricter allowlist/secret-scanning pass before any outbound comment. Treat all execution output as sensitive unless explicitly classified safe." } ], "summary": "Assumption: first-party JS under /Users/masayuki/.claude/auto-task was reviewed; vendored node_modules and non-JS wrappers were not scored. Key issues: the main risk is not classic SQLi/XSS/CSRF but unsafe autonomous execution policy around trusted issue authors, keyword-based task classification, and a weakened verification gate. Proposed approach: harden classification to deny by default, add a separate policy engine before delegate execution, and remove verification-time keyword obfuscation. Security considerations: no direct SQL injection surface was found, no meaningful XSS/CSRF surface was found in the reviewed JS, no hardcoded secrets were found in first-party JS, and classic shell command injection is partly mitigated by spawn() with argument arrays; however, LLM-mediated execution remains a high-impact control risk. Implementation complexity: medium overall; classification/policy hardening is medium, verification redesign is medium, and output-handling/redaction changes are low-to-medium." }
